events.label: reasoning
events.text: We are asked to assess drywall screw spacing that "exceeded" (presumably too far apart). The work is at rough-complete stage. Reference: GA-216 (Gypsum Association standard for application and finishing of gypsum panel products) and IRC (International Residential Code). The scenario says screw spacing exceeded. In drywall installation, screws must be spaced per manufacturer and code. Typically, for walls, screws are spaced 12" on center for ceilings and 16" for walls? Actually, GA-216 specifies: For single-layer application on ceilings, fasteners 12" o.c. on perimeter and 12" o.c. in field; for walls, 16" o.c. on perimeter and 16" o.c. in field? Wait, let's recall: GA-216 Table 1: For single-layer application, on ceilings, fasteners (screws) spaced 12" o.c. on perimeter and 12" o.c. in field; on walls, 16" o.c. on perimeter and 16" o.c. in field. But if screw spacing exceeded, that means they are too far apart, which is non-compliant. IRC R702.3.5 references GA-216. So it's a fail. Risk: medium? If screws are too far apart, drywall may sag or crack, but not immediate collapse. So risk medium. Work stage: rough-complete. Component conditions: installed-defective (because spacing is wrong) and non-compliant. Percent complete: maybe 80%? Drywall hung but not taped/finished. Value: none. Confidence: high. Findings: "Screw spacing exceeds maximum allowed per GA-216." Actions: "Remove and reinstall screws at proper spacing, or add additional screws." Rationale: "Excessive screw spacing can lead to sagging, nail pops, and cracking. GA-216 and IRC require specific fastener spacing." References: "GA-216 Table 1, IRC R702.3.5". So JSON output.

}
